steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks how the narrator characterizes the Seine. The key contrast is that the river is blocked or confined at the edge of Paris, then spreads out and becomes peaceful in the countryside.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘As the journey progressed from Paris to the countryside, the waters of the Seine gradually cleared.’: Wrong. Water clarity is never mentioned. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘The journey along the Seine is mostly peaceful, but the current intensifies briefly under the Point du Jour.’: Wrong. This reverses the passage's contrast. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The waters of the Seine are more confined and rough in Paris than they are in the countryside.’: Best supported. The river is blocked or confined near Paris, then regains space and becomes peaceful in the countryside. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘The Seine's loveliness is surpassed only by that of Paris.’: Wrong. The narrator's language favors the countryside river. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
